AC: Each of the 7 items is implemented or split into its own linked todo. State renames land in BOTH todo.py and SKILL.md/skill refs. Search changes keep vector recall (field-scoped boost is additive). item 3 deduped against any pre-existing state-search todo.

Body.raw:
Grooming backlog for the todo.py / todo_web.py tooling (repo: jovlinger/utils,
skills/projectmanagement/todos). Captured as pre-init; needs grooming/breakdown
before work. Items below are the raw requirements as stated.

1. Rename states (nouns, not gerunds). Rename pre-init (current state key `pre`)
   -> GROOM, and `init` -> READY. Other states are fine as-is. Update the skill
   references / SKILL.md too, not just the code. (VALID_STATES in todo.py already
   carries a "# rename to ready" note on `init`.) General principle: prefer nouns
   over gerunds for state names.

2. Search weighting. When the query includes a field-scoped term like `todo:xxx`
   or `sha:xxx`, weight matches on those fields very highly. Still run the vector
   search as well -- the field-scoped boost is on top of vector recall, not
   instead of it.

3. State-match search terms. Support `state:` search terms using the existing
   filter grammar (see STATE_SET_ALIASES / parse_state_filter: `+` = union,
   `-` = difference, `,` = `+`). Single states like `state:ready`, expressions
   with a shared prefix like `state:ready+wait`, and named groups like
   `state:active` and `state:workable-groom`. NOTE: there may already be a todo
   about this -- check and dedupe/link before implementing.

4. Priority: add a new priority field on the todo.

5. Sort terms. For some fields, support special search terms `desc` / `asc` to
   sort results by that field.

6. Search-box value model. The search box holds space-separated values of two
   kinds: (a) free text -- any token NOT matching `key:val` syntax -- which is
   equivalent to `text:"..."`; and (b) `key:val` terms, some of which have
   special handling (e.g. sorting). A `key:val` whose key is not recognized is
   text-ified (folded back into the free-text search).

7. Web tab title. The browser tab title should always be `todo web:<port>`
   regardless of what is currently being viewed.

Summary.raw: todo tool grooming: state renames (nouns), weighted/field-scoped search, state-filter terms, priority field, sort terms, stable web tab title
